themeWords.meaning: face, presence; in front of, before

sentenceTheme: A repentant community pleads for God to come down in holy power, remembering His past wonders and confessing their sins while appealing for mercy.

verses.text: Oh that thou wouldest rend the heavens, that thou wouldest come down, that the mountains might flow down at thy presence,

highlightedWords.meaning: heavens, sky, abode of the stars

verses.text: As when the melting fire burneth, the fire causeth the waters to boil, to make thy name known to thine adversaries, that the nations may tremble at thy presence!
When thou didst terrible things which we looked not for, thou camest down, the mountains flowed down at thy presence.
For since the beginning of the world men have not heard, nor perceived by the ear, neither hath the eye seen, O God, beside thee, what he hath prepared for him that waiteth for him.

highlightedWords.meaning: God, god; mighty one (plural of majesty when used of the true God)
to wait for, hope, look eagerly

verses.text: Thou meetest him that rejoiceth and worketh righteousness, those that remember thee in thy ways: behold, thou art wroth; for we have sinned: in those is continuance, and we shall be saved.

highlightedWords.meaning: to sin, miss the mark, offend

verses.text: But we are all as an unclean thing, and all our righteousnesses are as filthy rags; and we all do fade as a leaf; and our iniquities, like the wind, have taken us away.
And there is none that calleth upon thy name, that stirreth up himself to take hold of thee: for thou hast hid thy face from us, and hast consumed us, because of our iniquities.
But now, O LORD, thou art our father; we are the clay, and thou our potter; and we all are the work of thy hand.

highlightedWords.meaning: the personal name of the God of Israel (YHWH)
to form, fashion; a potter (as one who forms clay)

verses.text: Be not wroth very sore, O LORD, neither remember iniquity for ever: behold, see, we beseech thee, we are all thy people.

highlightedWords.meaning: to remember, recall, call to mind; to mention

verses.text: Thy holy cities are a wilderness, Zion is a wilderness, Jerusalem a desolation.

highlightedWords.meaning: Zion, a mountain of Jerusalem; the city or its people
Jerusalem, the chief city of Judah/Israel

verses.text: Our holy and our beautiful house, where our fathers praised thee, is burned up with fire: and all our pleasant things are laid waste.
Wilt thou refrain thyself for these things, O LORD? wilt thou hold thy peace, and afflict us very sore?

highlightedWords.meaning: the personal name of the God of Israel (YHWH)
